user_query:
I keep a couple of draft emails on my laptop that I use for quick updates to dispatch and check-ins with my family. I also have a simple mail-merge script and a config file that define how placeholders and the subject line should work.

Please do the following using only the files in this workspace and save all results locally:

1) Inspect all message drafts under input/drafts/ and process every *.md file you find. Inspect config/email_config.yaml to read my signature block, the allowed placeholder variables, and placeholder style. Inspect scripts/mailer.py to confirm the template rules it expects (e.g., that the first non-empty line must start with "Subject:" and that placeholders are Jinja2-style {{ var }}).

2) For each draft:
- Rewrite the body for tone, clarity, and brevity while preserving the intent and any scheduling details. Keep the body at or below 120 words.
- Use an appropriately brief tone: professional/concise for shipper/dispatch updates (e.g., shipper_update.md) and warm/conversational for family messages (e.g., family_checkin.md).
- Ensure the first non-empty line begins with "Subject:" followed by a concise subject. Add or fix it if missing.
- Standardize all placeholders to Jinja2-style variables that exactly match the allowed_vars defined in config/email_config.yaml and used in scripts/mailer.py (e.g., rename eta->delivery_eta, DriverName->driver_name, truckId->truck_id, currentLocation->current_location, carePkgTracking->care_pkg_tracking). Do not introduce any variables outside the allowed set.
- If a draft mentions food, lunch, or a care package in plain text, replace that sentence with a single placeholder reference that uses an allowed variable (e.g., {{ lunch_note }} for lunch remarks) positioned naturally.
- Append the multi-line signature from config/email_config.yaml at the end of the body (retain its placeholders; do not hardcode values). Remove any duplicate or conflicting sign-offs from the original draft.

3) Save the rewritten templates to output/revised_templates/ with the same filenames as their inputs.

4) Create output/report.json summarizing what you changed and verifying compatibility with the script and config. For each processed file, include at minimum: file (input path), output_file, original_word_count, revised_word_count, subject_present (boolean), variables_found (in the original), variables_fixed (a mapping of original->updated names you changed), uses_allowed_variables_only (boolean on the revised file), signature_appended_from_config (boolean), and notes (brief reasoning on tone changes). Also include top-level fields allowed_variables (from config) and script_template_rules_detected with keys subject_first_line (boolean) and placeholder_style (string) based on what you observed in scripts/mailer.py.

Inputs are in input/drafts/, config/email_config.yaml, and scripts/mailer.py. Outputs must be written to output/revised_templates/ (templates) and output/report.json (report). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
